 ***Group 3 \- Presentation***

***1.Analysis of business processes***

***Check-in process***

The check-in process governs the controlled admission of vehicles into the parking facility. It begins when a vehicle arrives at an entry gate and ends when the vehicle has been assigned a slot and a parking session has been created. This process is the primary entry point and must execute with minimal latency to prevent gate congestion.

**Actors Involved**

* Parking Staff: primary executor of the check-in flow

* Parking User / Driver: subject of the process, provides vehicle information

* System: performs slot availability checks, session creation, and ticket generation

**Preconditions**

* The system is online and authenticated staff session is active

* At least one slot compatible with the vehicle type is in Available status

* The vehicle has not already been checked in (no duplicate active sessions)

**Postconditions**

* A parking session record exists with TimeIn, vehicle license plate, vehicle type, and assigned slot ID

* The assigned slot status has been updated from Available to Occupied

* A physical or digital parking ticket has been issued to the driver

**Process Flow**

| Step | Actor | Action | Output | Exception Case |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | User / Driver | Vehicle arrives at entry gate and presents itself for check-in. | Identify vehicle presence, begin check-in process | — |
| 2 | System / Staff | Scan license plate using camera or manual entry, identify vehicle type (car, motorcycle, truck, etc.). | License plate & vehicle type captured | Unreadable plate \=\> staff manually enters license plate |
| 3 | System | Check if available slots exist for this vehicle type and the corresponding floor/zone. | Availability boolean returned | No slots available \=\> system alerts staff, driver redirected to alternative area |
| 4 | System | Verify no existing active session for this license plate (prevent duplicates). | Unique vehicle confirmed | Duplicate found \=\> alert error; staff resolves problem manually before commencing |
| 5 | System | Auto-assign a slot from the optimal available pool (AI-assisted: balance occupancy across sectors/floors). | Slot ID assigned | Auto-assign fails \=\> staff assigns slot manually |
| 6 | System | Create parking session record: TimeIn, VehicleType, LicensePlate, SlotID, Status=Occupied, GateID. | Session record persisted in DB | DB write error \=\> retry / alert system admin |
| 7 | System | Update slot status from Available to Occupied; decrement available count for vehicle type. | Real-time slot inventory updated | Sync failure \=\> flag for reconciliation |
| 8 | System / Staff | Generate and issue parking ticket (physical printout or digital QR code) containing: ticket ID, TimeIn, slot, vehicle type. | Ticket delivered to driver | Printer failure \=\> digital ticket issued as fallback |
| 9 | System / Staff | Open entry gate; vehicle proceeds to designated floor/zone. Process ends. | END — vehicle inside | Gate malfunction \=\> manual override |

***2.Check-out & Payment process***

The check-out process manages vehicle departure, parking fee computation, payment collection, and slot release. It is the financial closure of the parking session opened during check-in. Being critical for security and congestion prevention, this process must handle multiple payment scenarios and exception cases (lost tickets, incorrect vehicle details, overtime) while ensuring the slot is accurately returned to Available status upon successful exit.

**Actors Involved**

* Parking Staff: validates exit, collects payment, resolves disputes

* Parking User / Driver: presents ticket, pays fee

* System: retrieves session, calculates fee, updates records

**Preconditions**

* An active parking session exists, linked to a valid ticket ID or license plate

* A pricing rule applicable to the vehicle type and parking duration is configured

* Staff is authenticated and has check-out permission

**Postconditions**

* Parking session is closed with TimeOut recorded

* Parking fee has been calculated and payment confirmed

* Assigned slot status updated from Occupied to Available

* Revenue record updated for financial reconciliation

**Process Flow**

| Step | Actor | Action | Output | Exception Case |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Driver | Vehicle arrives at exit gate. Driver presents parking ticket (physical or digital QR code) to the staff or reader terminal. | Ticket received | Lost ticket \=\> staff performs identity verification, continue check-out process with lost ticket fee per policy |
| 2 | System | CheckTicket? Validate ticket ID: search for active session matching the ticket. Verify ticket is not expired, forged, or already used. | Session record retrieved | Invalid/unmatched ticket \=\> staff manually resolves problem, continue check-out process |
| 3 | System | Capture TimeOut \= current timestamp. Compute Duration \= TimeOut minus TimeIn (in minutes/hours). | Duration value calculated | Clock sync error \=\> system alert, admin and staff review |
| 4 | System | Calculate fee depending on type, duration and policies. Handle special cases if applicable. | Parking fee amount calculated | No matching pricing rule \=\> default rate applied; flag for manager review |
| 5 | Staff | Present fee to driver. Collect payment via accepted methods (cash, card, online payment). | Payment received confirmation | Insufficient payment \=\> negotiation / hold vehicle, driver disputes fee |
| 6 | System | Close parking session: record TimeOut, Duration, FeeCharged, PaymentMethod, StaffID. Status set to Closed. | Session record closed | DB write failure \=\> retry, record in error log |
| 7 | System | Update slot status: Occupied to Available. Increment AvailableCount for vehicle type. | Slot freed | Slot update fails \=\> reconciliation job flags failure |
| 8 | System | Generate receipt (digital or printed). Log revenue entry for shift reconciliation. | Receipt issued, revenue log updated | — |
| 9 | System / Staff | Open exit gate. Vehicle departs. Process ends. | END — vehicle exited | Gate malfunction \=\> manual gate opening |

**Fee Calculation Logic**

The pricing engine must be flexible to support different vehicle types, time bands, and special policies:

* Different hourly/daily rates for bikes, motorbikes, cars, trucks, and oversized vehicles.

* Rates may vary by duration of stay.

* Extended stays (overnight, multiple days) trigger different rate schedules.

* Optional dynamic pricing based on occupancy and different times of the day.

* If a ticket is lost, a penalty or maximum daily rate may be applied.

* Additional surcharges for vehicles exceeding allowed duration or parked in wrong zones.

  ***Parking Management***  
  Parking space management encompasses all processes related to the configuration, monitoring, and real-time maintenance of parking infrastructure, including sectors (floors/zones), individual slots, and their statuses. This process is an essential and continuous background operation that underpins both check-in and check-out accuracy, ensuring every square foot of the parking space is utilized efficiently.

**Actors Involved**

* Parking Facility Manager: defines and configures sectors, vehicle type allocations, and slot policies

* Parking Staff: updates individual slot statuses in response to operational events

* System: auto-updates slot status on session creation/closure; triggers alerts on anomalies

**Configuration & Management of different sectors in the parking space**

A foundational and necessary process which defines the physical and logical structure of the parking facility. Firstly, the Parking Facility Manager configures the entire parking space layout, including these individual actions:

* Manager creates sectors (floors, zones, wings). Each sector is assigned a vehicle type affinity to separate different vehicle types.

* Individual slots are created within each sector.

* Manager configures which floors accept which vehicle types. This constrains the slot assignment engine during check-in.

* Manager sets total capacity per vehicle type and per sector. System enforces capacity limits during check-in.

Each slot progresses through a defined set of statuses. Accurate status tracking is critical for correct availability reporting and slot assignment:

| Status | Description | Triggered By | Can Transition To |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Available | Slot is empty and ready for assignment. | Check-out completion, Admin unlock | Occupied, Reserved, Maintenance, Locked |
| Occupied | Vehicle is currently parked in this slot. | Successful check-in | Available (on check-out) |
| Reserved | Slot is pre-booked by a driver for a future time window. | Advance booking confirmed | Available (if cancelled/expired), Occupied |
| Maintenance | Slot is under repair or inspection; not assignable. | Manager / Staff manual update | Available (when maintenance is completed) |
| Temporarily Locked | Slot is temporarily withheld from assignment (emergency, special tasks). | Manager manual action | Available (on unlock) |

**AI-assisted Space Optimization**

An AI slot allocation module is typically recommended to enhance operational efficiency. At full implementation, it can:

* Evaluate multiple factors (vehicle type, slot proximity to elevator/exit, current sector load, historical flow patterns) during check-in to assign the optimal slot rather than the first available.

* Distribute vehicles across sectors to minimize congestion at ramps and elevators, reducing search time for drivers.

* Analyze historical check-in/check-out patterns to predict peak periods and pre-position reserved slots accordingly.

* Automatically verify vehicle type against assigned zone during check-in to flag potential mismatches before the vehicle proceeds.

 

***User Management***  
User management controls all aspects of system identity: who can access the system, under what role, and with what permissions. It is the security gateway through which every other process must pass. The process spans account creation, authentication, role-based access control, and account lifecycle management.

**Actors Involved**

* System Administrator: creates accounts, assigns roles, manages configuration

* Parking Manager: has elevated access, may be managed by admin

* Parking Staff: standard operational role, access limited to check-in/check-out functions

* System: authenticates credentials, enforces access control on every request

**Authentication Flow**

| Step | Actor | Action | Output | Exception Case |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | User (any role) | Navigate to login page. Enter username and password credentials. | Credentials submitted | — |
| 2 | System | Validate credentials against stored password record. Check account active status. | Verification result: Succeed / Fail | — |
| 3a | System | \[Failure\] Authentication failed: invalid credentials or inactive account. Display Alert Error message. Increment failed attempt counter. | Error displayed, user returned to login | Repeat failures \=\> account lockout, Admin notified |
| 3b | System | \[Success\] Credentials valid. Proceed to role identification. | Authenticated session token issued | Session token failure \=\> retry |
| 4 | System | IsRole? — Query the user's assigned role(s). Determine access level: Staff or Admin/Manager. | Role resolved, grant access based on role | Role not assigned \=\> Admin alert, deny by default until resolution |
| 5a | System | \[Staff Role\] Grant access to Check-In and Check-Out functions only. Redirect to staff operational dashboard. | Staff dashboard loaded | — |
| 5b | System | \[Admin/Manager Role\] Grant access to all system functions including management, reporting, configuration, and user administration. | Full admin dashboard loaded | — |

**Account Lifecycle Management**

The System Administrator manages the full lifecycle of user accounts:

* Admin creates accounts for managers and staff. Defines username, initial password, assigned role(s), associated gate/sector access.

* Admin assigns or modifies roles at any time. Changes take effect on next login or session refresh.

* Admin can reset passwords. Users may change their own passwords per policy. Failed login lockout managed here.

* Admin can suspend an account (e.g., staff on leave). Suspended accounts cannot log in, active sessions are terminated.

* Accounts are deactivated (soft delete) to preserve audit history. Hard deletion only with allowance.

* All authentication events, role changes, and admin actions are logged with timestamp, actor ID, and action type.

  ***Side Processes***

Outside the main four processes, these are some of the important side processes that keep the system going smoothly.

**Report Process**

The reporting process provides Parking Managers with operational intelligence for decision making and revenue reconciliation. This would include these following report types:

* Vehicle entry/exit log: timestamped log of all sessions (check-in and check-out) with vehicle type, slot, duration, and fee.

* Revenue report: daily, weekly, and monthly revenue summaries by vehicle type, shift, staff, and gate. Supports shift reconciliation.

* Occupancy rate report: average and peak occupancy by sector, floor, vehicle type, and time band. Feeds ai optimization engine if implemented.

* Peak hour analysis: histogram of entry/exit volumes by hour and day of week. Used for staffing and capacity planning.

* Exception report: log of all anomalies: lost tickets, incorrect plates, overtime, wrong-area incidents, fee overrides, and unpaid vehicles.

* Slot utilization report: statistics per slot including total sessions, average duration, revenue contribution, and downtime (maintenance periods).

**System Administration**

Beyond user account management, the System Administrator handles several technical background processes

| Admin Process | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| System Configuration | Configure global parameters: operating hours, maximum session duration, lockout thresholds, payment gateway credentials, and API integrations. |
| Hardware Integration Management | Manage integrations with gate controllers, license plate cameras, ticket printers, and payment terminals. Monitor device health. |
| Data Backup & Recovery | Scheduled data backup processes. Recovery procedure activation in case of system failure. Data retention policy enforcement. |
| Audit Log Review | Periodic review of security and operational audit logs for compliance, anomaly detection, and forensic investigation. |
| System Notification Management | Configure alerts for critical events: slot inventory below threshold, system errors, unauthorized access attempts, and revenue discrepancies. |

***2.Draw and describe the business workflow steps***  
![flowchart (3).drawio][image1]

***3.Identify the actors/users who interact with the system.***  
**Parking facility manager:**

* Manage parking building  
  * Manage vehicle types  
  * Manage vehicle type by floor  
  * Manage parking slot  
  * Manage price  
  * Manage policies  
  * View reports  
  * Handle exceptional situations  
- **Parking staff:**  
  * Assist vehicle entry processing  
  * Create parking session  
  * Assist vehicle exit processing  
  * Handle exceptional situations  
- **Parking user/driver:**  
  * View parking information  
  * Park in turns  
  * Book parking slot  
  * Pay parking fees  
  * Report issues  
- **System administrator:**  
  * Manage user accounts  
  * Manage roles  
  * Manage systems
